SNDK.headline: Micron’s $100 Billion Backlog Could Reset How the Market Values Memory
SNDK.description: Micron delivered record earnings with $41.46B in Q3 revenue and 84.9% gross margin, surpassing Nvidia's margins. The company secured $100B in multi-year customer contracts, transforming it from cyclical chipmaker to predictable revenue generator. Q4 guidance of ~$50B revenue and $30-32 EPS significantly exceeded expectations, driving the stock up 15.95% and prompting Wall Street to raise price targets toward $2,000+.

SNDK.headline: Why Sandisk Stock Soared Today
SNDK.description: Sandisk stock surged 11.2% after rival Micron beat earnings expectations, reporting $25.11 per share versus forecasted $20.78 and quadrupling sales to $41.5 billion. With Sandisk's own earnings expected to more than double to $33.72 per share in August, investors are optimistic the company will follow Micron's strong performance. Both companies are benefiting from AI-driven demand and locking in high profit margins through long-term customer agreements.

SNDK.headline: Micron and Qualcomm Show AI Demand Is Broadening Beyond GPUs
SNDK.description: U.S. stocks rallied on Thursday following strong earnings and guidance from Micron Technology and Qualcomm, which revived confidence in the AI trade despite sticky inflation. Micron's fiscal Q3 results significantly beat expectations with EPS of $25.11 versus forecasts of $20.78 and revenue of $41.46 billion. Qualcomm doubled its 2029 non-handset revenue forecast to $40 billion, signaling that AI demand is expanding beyond GPUs into data centers and other sectors.

SNDK.headline: Sandisk Stock Is Up 720% in 2026. Is the Rally Still Worth Chasing?
SNDK.description: Sandisk, spun off from Western Digital in February 2025, has surged over 5,200% since its separation and 720% in 2026 alone. The company has transformed from a slow-growth memory chipmaker into a high-growth AI play, driven by data center upgrades and its 256TB enterprise SSDs for AI workloads. Despite the massive rally, analysts expect 167% revenue growth and 2,089% EPS growth in fiscal 2026, with the stock trading at only 10-11x forward earnings—suggesting further upside potential if the marke

SNDK.headline: Can Micron Stop the AI Selloff After Korea’s Leverage Flush?
SNDK.description: South Korea's leverage flush triggered a tech selloff, with Samsung and SK Hynix falling over 12% and spreading to US memory stocks like Micron. The article examines whether this represents a temporary deleveraging event or the start of a deeper correction, with Micron's earnings serving as a critical test of AI demand fundamentals and the sustainability of the semiconductor rally.

SNDK.headline: MEXC May Stock Futures Volume Rises 105%, MU Volume Surges 1,002% as AI Storage Momentum Builds
SNDK.description: MEXC released May 2026 TradFi Futures trading data showing significant growth across U.S. stock-related products. Stock Futures trading volume increased 105% month-over-month, with semiconductor stocks like Micron (MU) surging over 1,000% and SanDisk (SNDK) up 757%. Index and ETF products also saw strong demand, with SPX500 and NAS100 becoming the most actively traded products. The platform expanded its global stock product matrix to cover over 140 equity-related assets and 160+ traditional fina

MU.headline: Nvidia’s Micron-Led Bounce Tests Confidence in the AI Capex Cycle
MU.description: Nvidia rebounded near $200 after Micron's record earnings reignited confidence in AI semiconductor demand. CEO Jensen Huang declared AI has entered a profitability era and confirmed the next-generation Vera Rubin architecture is in full production. However, the stock faces headwinds from rising competition, margin pressure from memory suppliers, geopolitical risks from China export controls, and uncertainty about whether AI capital spending can sustain current growth rates.

MU.headline: Micron’s $100 Billion Backlog Could Reset How the Market Values Memory
MU.description: Micron delivered record earnings with $41.46B in Q3 revenue and 84.9% gross margin, surpassing Nvidia's margins. The company secured $100B in multi-year customer contracts, transforming it from cyclical chipmaker to predictable revenue generator. Q4 guidance of ~$50B revenue and $30-32 EPS significantly exceeded expectations, driving the stock up 15.95% and prompting Wall Street to raise price targets toward $2,000+.

MU.headline: Micron Profits and Apple Prices Reveal AI’s Hidden Inflation Problem
MU.description: While AI is expected to boost productivity and lower inflation long-term, the massive investment in AI infrastructure is creating near-term inflationary pressures. Micron's strong earnings reflect booming demand for advanced chips, but supply constraints are forcing Apple to raise prices on Mac and iPad models. The Fed's preferred inflation gauge has climbed above 4%, complicating expectations for rate cuts in 2026.

MU.headline: Micron’s Record Sales Show the Memory Bottleneck Is Real
MU.description: Micron Technology reported record sales and earnings, demonstrating that the memory bottleneck is real and benefiting the memory and SSD industry. The author also discusses defense contractors developing advanced aerospace technology and recommends selling cryptocurrencies due to Bitcoin's significant decline and a strengthening U.S. dollar.

MU.headline: PCE Inflation Reached Its Highest Level in 3 Years. Here’s What Investors Need to Know.
MU.description: PCE inflation rose to 4.1% year-over-year in May, the highest in three years, driven primarily by rising oil prices from Middle East tensions. However, recent declines in oil prices following geopolitical developments suggest inflation may moderate. The Fed expects one rate hike this year, though uncertainty remains around future monetary policy under new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh.

MU.headline: Is Micron a Buy After Its Blowout Earnings Report?
MU.description: Micron Technology has emerged as a key beneficiary of the AI boom, reporting record quarterly revenue exceeding $41 billion and net income of $28 billion, both beating analyst estimates. The company signed 16 customer agreements through 2030 worth $22 billion in commitments, providing visibility on future revenue. With a stock gain of over 260% this year and a forward P/E ratio of 16x (lower than tech peers like Nvidia and Alphabet), analysts suggest the stock remains reasonably valued for growt

MU.headline: Why Sandisk Stock Soared Today
MU.description: Sandisk stock surged 11.2% after rival Micron beat earnings expectations, reporting $25.11 per share versus forecasted $20.78 and quadrupling sales to $41.5 billion. With Sandisk's own earnings expected to more than double to $33.72 per share in August, investors are optimistic the company will follow Micron's strong performance. Both companies are benefiting from AI-driven demand and locking in high profit margins through long-term customer agreements.

MU.headline: Micron Prints Perfect, and Now the Chart Has to Answer
MU.description: Micron delivered exceptional Q3 results with $41.5B revenue (up 346% YoY), 84.9% gross margin, and EPS of $25.11 beating expectations. The company guided Q4 to $50B revenue and secured $100B in long-term take-or-pay contracts through 2030, providing structural support. However, the stock's reaction remains uncertain as it trades within a rising channel, with the key question being whether the market views this as peak growth or a sign of continued acceleration.

MU.headline: Micron and Qualcomm Show AI Demand Is Broadening Beyond GPUs
MU.description: U.S. stocks rallied on Thursday following strong earnings and guidance from Micron Technology and Qualcomm, which revived confidence in the AI trade despite sticky inflation. Micron's fiscal Q3 results significantly beat expectations with EPS of $25.11 versus forecasts of $20.78 and revenue of $41.46 billion. Qualcomm doubled its 2029 non-handset revenue forecast to $40 billion, signaling that AI demand is expanding beyond GPUs into data centers and other sectors.

MU.headline: SpaceX (SPCX) Stock Is Plunging. Should You Buy the Dip?
MU.description: SpaceX shares have fallen nearly 20% from their June 16 peak after surging 67% at IPO. Despite the dip, the author recommends against buying, citing an extremely high price-to-sales ratio of 77 (compared to Nvidia's 20 and Micron's 24), lack of profitability, and concerns about planned debt and equity dilution. The stock is priced for perfection with no margin of safety.

APGE.headline: Halper Sadeh LLC is Investigating Whether APGE, LPRO, HUN, AVNS are Obtaining Fair Deals for their Shareholders
APGE.description: Investor rights law firm Halper Sadeh LLC is investigating four companies for potential securities law violations and breaches of fiduciary duties related to their proposed acquisitions. The firm is examining whether shareholders are receiving fair consideration and whether insider benefits may be limiting competing offers.

WEN.headline: Why Wendy's Stock Popped Today
WEN.description: Wendy's stock surged 25.66% on Wednesday, driven by retail investor interest on WallStreetBets and the appointment of new CEO Robert Wright and CFO Steve Cirulis, both turnaround experts from Potbelly. Despite the gains, the struggling burger chain faces significant headwinds including a 21.9% operating profit decline and 6.8% same-store sales drop in Q1.

WEN.headline: Stock Market Today, June 24: Micron Surges After Hours as Nasdaq Stems Losses
WEN.description: U.S. stock indexes showed mixed performance on June 24, 2026, with the Dow gaining 0.35% while the Nasdaq fell 0.43% amid semiconductor volatility. Micron Technology surged over 13% in after-hours trading following strong earnings and guidance. Other notable moves included Wendy's jumping 26%, Hertz tumbling 40% after a stock offering announcement, and Cerebras Systems crashing on its first earnings report as a public company. JPMorgan raised its year-end S&P 500 target to 7,800.
